claim: YOLO demand evidence dispatch already implements JSON key drift repair and fails closed with EVIDENCE_AGENT_INVALID_JSON blocker on invalid agent JSON. The implementation exists in a working tree with comprehensive test coverage.

evidence.summary: parseJsonCandidate and extractJsonObject implement JSON repair with four regex patterns: line ranges (42-88 to "42-88"), backslash escapes, drifted keys (" "summary"": to "summary":), and trailing commas. Returns repaired flag.
evidence.why: Core JSON repair logic that handles minor Claude key drift as specified in the demand.

evidence.summary: normalizeAgentResult returns EVIDENCE_AGENT_INVALID_JSON error code when parsed is null, sets verdict to blocked, and records parse error. Also validates evidence scope and returns EVIDENCE_SCOPE_REQUIRED when missing.
evidence.why: Fail-closed behavior that produces explicit blocker codes on invalid or incomplete agent JSON.

evidence.summary: Runtime extracts JSON from provider stdout, normalizes agent result, tracks json_repaired flag in provider_runs, and builds result.agent_results array.
evidence.why: Integration point where JSON repair is recorded and agent failures are captured.

evidence.summary: After all agents complete, inspects PRD readiness, adds BOUNDARY_PROJECT_MUTATION blockers if files changed outside allowed roots, sets result.status based on finalReadiness.prd_ready.
evidence.why: PRD readiness stays blocked when any blockers including EVIDENCE_AGENT_INVALID_JSON are present.

evidence.summary: evidenceScopeErrors validates each evidence record has declared scope (project, external, user, unknown). Returns list of error messages.
evidence.why: Evidence scope validation that blocks normalization when scope is missing.

evidence.summary: Test repairs minor Claude JSON key drift with three drift patterns: line range 42-88, escaped backslash in regex, and key drift " "summary"". Asserts result.status=pass and json_repaired=true.
evidence.why: Proves JSON repair functionality works and is recorded.

evidence.summary: Test invalid agent JSON returns stdout='not json'. Asserts result.status=blocked, agent_results have error_code=EVIDENCE_AGENT_INVALID_JSON, and readiness.blockers contains EVIDENCE_AGENT_INVALID_JSON.
evidence.why: Proves fail-closed behavior with explicit blocker code on unrecoverable JSON.

evidence.summary: Test evidence without scope field. Asserts result.status=blocked, error_code=EVIDENCE_SCOPE_REQUIRED, and readiness.blockers contains EVIDENCE_SCOPE_REQUIRED.
evidence.why: Proves evidence scope validation produces explicit blocker when scope is missing.

evidence.summary: evidenceScopeDeclarationIssues checks evidenceRecordHasDeclaredScope, returns EVIDENCE_SCOPE_REQUIRED blocker with message requiring scope declaration.
evidence.why: Router-level evidence scope validation that produces explicit blockers.

evidence.summary: evidenceResultBlockingIssues checks error_code, missing items, recommendation/verdict/status, and returns blocker codes including EVIDENCE_AGENT_BLOCKED and EVIDENCE_AGENT_CLARIFICATION_REQUIRED.
evidence.why: Router-level validation that converts agent issues into explicit blockers.

assumptions: Dirty working tree mentioned in status_quo refers to uncommitted changes visible in git status, not a broken state requiring repair.
Stress-testing with real Claude -p agents is intended as manual validation, not an automated test gap.
risks: If the working tree is dirty due to merge conflicts or local modifications not reflected in tests, stress-testing might reveal runtime failures not caught by the test suite.
JSON repair patterns might not cover all Claude key drift variants, leading to EVIDENCE_AGENT_INVALID_JSON blockers that should be recoverable.

result.notes: Claim verified: evidence-dispatch.ts implements JSON repair (lines 333-340), records repairs (line 557), fails closed with EVIDENCE_AGENT_INVALID_JSON (line 371), validates scope (lines 150-157, 407-418), and keeps PRD blocked (lines 598-599). All behaviors have test coverage (lines 496-601).
